stories.title: SpaceX to acquire AI coding platform Cursor for $60 billion
stories.author: Samuel Axon
bullet_points: SpaceX is reportedly acquiring the AI coding platform Cursor.
The acquisition is valued at a massive $60 billion.
This signals a major industry shift as SpaceX integrates advanced AI coding capabilities.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The pipeline indicates its primary use case is text generation.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags such as 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and evaluation of Meta's latest open-source LLM iterations.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and utilizes transformers and safetensors.
It is associated with the deepseek_v3 lineage, indicating relevance in the LLM space.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating generative AI focus.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher quality outputs, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing local AI inference.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, signaling a push for hardware-specific AI optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
This emphasizes a trend toward private, on-device AI capabilities, contrasting with cloud-based models.

stories.title: Snap is finally about to ship AR glasses — and they cost a fortune
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Snap is launching its first public augmented reality (AR) glasses, named 'Specs'.
The device is positioned as a 'wearable computer' and will carry a high price tag of $2,195.
Preorders are available with a $200 refundable deposit, with shipments expected this fall in key markets.

stories.title: SoftMoE: Soft Differentiable Routing for Mixture-of-Experts in LLMs
stories.author: Marcin Kurdziel
bullet_points: Introduces SoftMoE, a method to replace discrete routing in Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) LLMs with a differentiable approach.
Addresses the inefficiency of traditional top-$k$ routing by using a truncated soft top-$k$ LapSum relaxation.
Aims to improve computational efficiency in large language models while maintaining the benefits of sparse MoE architectures.

stories.title: GameCraft-Bench: Can Agents Build Playable Games End-to-End in a Real Game Engine?
stories.author: Benyou Wang
bullet_points: Introduces GameCraft-Bench, a benchmark for testing end-to-end game generation by AI agents.
Addresses the complex challenge of translating natural language specifications into fully playable, interactive game systems.
Highlights that game generation requires coordinating multiple components within a game engine (scripts, assets, rendering, runtime).

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It strengthens Ollama's MLX engine specifically for Apple silicon users.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: All the latest news on Android 17, Wear OS 7, and Android XR
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Android 17 introduces multitasking features like floating 'Bubble' app windows and a 50/50 split gaming mode for foldables.
Wear OS 7 focuses on improving smartwatches with Live Updates and enhanced battery life.
The updates pave the way for new Android XR smart glasses, signaling continued development in spatial computing.

stories.title: Google launches Wear OS 7 with Live Updates and a battery life boost
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google is rolling out Wear OS 7 to Pixel Watch 2, 3, and 4.
The key new feature is 'Live Updates,' which tracks real-time events.
Live Updates will sync across Wear OS devices for things like sports scores.

stories.title: Qualcomm’s latest chip hints that more powerful smart glasses could be on the way
stories.author: Victoria Song
bullet_points: Qualcomm is developing the Snapdragon Reality Elite chip to power next-generation XR devices.
The chip development signals progress in the nascent smart glasses market.
The announcement was made at the Augmented World Expo, with early hands-on demos available.

stories.title: The Google / Xreal Aura XR glasses are now available to preorder
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: The Google/Xreal Aura XR glasses are now available for preorder.
The device is priced starting at $99 and is positioned as the second Android XR device.
A full launch across several major international markets is anticipated this Fall.

stories.title: Anthropic "pauses" token-based billing for its Claude Agent SDK
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Anthropic has temporarily halted the implementation of token-based billing for its Claude Agent SDK.
The planned billing change was expected to significantly raise costs for heavy users.
This pause suggests a response to potential user backlash or a reassessment of the pricing model.

stories.title: Pentagon boasts of using AI to write reports mandated by Congress
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: The Pentagon is reportedly utilizing AI, specifically generative AI, to draft reports required by Congress.
The military claims a substantial adoption rate, with 1.5 million personnel using generative AI tools.
This highlights the increasing integration and operational deployment of generative AI within major government/defense agencies.

stories.title: Trump admin abandons fight against wind energy as clean energy output surges
stories.author: Aman Azhar, Inside Climate News
bullet_points: The Trump administration's efforts to impede wind and solar energy have been undermined by legal challenges.
Clean energy output is reportedly surging, indicating a shift in energy production trends.
The story highlights the influence of legal victories on energy policy debates.

stories.title: LLM Consumer Behavior Theory: Foundations of a Novel Research Field
stories.author: David Martens
bullet_points: LLMs are evolving into autonomous agents capable of making consumption decisions for users.
This trend necessitates a new theoretical framework, 'LLM Consumer Behavior Theory,' to study agentic markets.
The research integrates classical and behavioral economics with NLP advancements to address this shift.

stories.title: C2FL: Clustered Continual Federated Learning under Spatial and Temporal Drift
stories.author: Lukas Esterle
bullet_points: Addresses challenges in scaling Collective Adaptive Systems (CAS) using machine learning.
Focuses on Federated Learning (FL) to handle privacy-sensitive, decentralized data.
Introduces 'C2FL' to manage data heterogeneity caused by spatial and temporal drift in mobile node environments.

stories.title: A T-API-Compliant ReAct Agentic Loop for Optical Networks: Generic vs. Domain-Specific Tool Abstractions
stories.author: Carlos Natalino
bullet_points: Introduces a T-API-compliant ReAct agentic loop specifically designed for intent-driven management of optical networks.
Demonstrates that domain-specific composite tools significantly outperform generic tools in performance.
Achieves 90% oracle-validated correctness while reducing token usage by a factor of three compared to generic approaches.

stories.title: STAR: SpatioTemporal Adaptive Reward Allocation for Text-to-Image RL Post-Training
stories.author: Jian Luan
bullet_points: Introduces STAR, a new method for SpatioTemporal Adaptive Reward Allocation in Text-to-Image RL Post-Training.
Addresses a limitation in current methods where a single scalar reward is applied uniformly across the entire image generation trajectory.
Recognizes that text-to-image generation has inherent temporal (denoising steps) and spatial structures that require granular reward allocation.

stories.title: SegDINO: Introducing Multi-Scale Structure into DINO for Efficient Medical Image Segmentation
stories.author: Lei Zhu
bullet_points: SegDINO addresses the challenge of applying self-supervised DINO models to image segmentation efficiently.
The research posits that incorporating multi-scale structure into DINO features is more critical than increasing decoder capacity.
The framework integrates a DINOv3 backbone to create an efficient segmentation model for medical images.

stories.title: A Neuro-Symbolic Approach to Strategy Synthesis for Strategic Logics
stories.author: Luca Rizzuti
bullet_points: Addresses the computational bottleneck in Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) strategy synthesis, a core challenge in AI.
Proposes a novel neuro-symbolic framework by integrating Large Language Models (LLMs) into the model-checking pipeline.
The LLM functions as a strategy-generation oracle, proposing candidate strategies for formal verification.

stories.title: C2FL: Clustered Continual Federated Learning under Spatial and Temporal Drift
stories.author: Lukas Esterle
bullet_points: Addresses challenges in scaling Collective Adaptive Systems (CAS) using machine learning.
Focuses on Federated Learning (FL) to handle privacy-sensitive, locally sensed data.
Proposes 'Clustered Continual Federated Learning' (C2FL) to manage spatial and temporal data drift in mobile node environments.

stories.title: Half a Link can Be Enough to Predict a Whole Link: Understanding Generalization in Knowledge Graph Foundation Models
stories.author: Steffen Staab
bullet_points: The paper investigates the generalization capabilities of Knowledge Graph Foundation Models (KGFMs), which are noted for their zero-shot learning ability.
It introduces the concept of 'half-links' to analyze how KGFMs perform when only partial information about a link is available.
The research reveals that the generalization performance of these models is not uniform across different types of partially seen links.

stories.title: Differential Privacy of Gaussian Process Posterior Sampling
stories.author: Tomasz Maciazek
bullet_points: The research addresses the differential privacy (DP) of releasing posterior sample paths from Gaussian Processes (GPs).
It demonstrates that the inherent randomness of posterior sampling provides DP guarantees without needing external noise addition.
The study derives explicit Rényi-DP bounds, separating leakage related to the posterior mean from that of the posterior covariance.

stories.title: Beyond Visual Cues: CoT-Enhanced Reasoning for Semi-supervised Medical Image Segmentation
stories.author: Yi Zhou
bullet_points: The research addresses semi-supervised medical image segmentation, a key problem in medical AI.
It critiques existing methods for relying too heavily on visual pattern matching, which fails in complex clinical scenarios.
The proposed solution incorporates Chain-of-Thought (CoT) enhancement to improve reasoning beyond mere visual cues.

stories.title: KANLib -- An Modular, Extensible and Fast Kolmogorov-Arnold Network Implementation
stories.author: Gregor Schiele
bullet_points: Introduces KANLib, a new framework for implementing Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs).
KANs are presented as a promising alternative to traditional MLPs, offering better interpretability and expressiveness.
The framework addresses previous practical limitations of KANs, such as high computational costs and framework inconsistency.

stories.title: VoidPadding: Let [VOID] Handle Padding in Masked Diffusion Language Models so that [EOS] Can Focus on Semantic Termination
stories.author: Alex Lamb
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical issue in Masked Diffusion Language Models (MDLMs) where the End-of-Sequence token ([EOS]) serves a dual role as both a semantic terminator and a padding token.
This dual role is identified as the root cause of 'EOS overflow' when using large-block decoding in instruction tuning.
The proposed solution, VoidPadding, aims to decouple these roles by using a special [VOID] token to handle padding, allowing [EOS] to focus purely on semantic termination.

stories.title: Fine-tuning LLMs for Passive Depression Severity Estimation from AI Mental Health Dialogue
stories.author: Caitlin A. Stamatis
bullet_points: The research proposes using Large Language Models (LLMs) to passively estimate depression severity (PHQ-9 scores) from AI mental health dialogue transcripts.
This approach aims to overcome low completion rates and response bias associated with traditional, manual symptom monitoring tools.
It represents a move toward scalable, real-time mental health monitoring using conversational AI data.

stories.title: Learning task-specific subspaces via interventional post-training of speech foundation models
stories.author: Jon Barker
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of general-purpose speech foundation models encoding information too broadly for specific downstream tasks.
A novel post-training refinement method using interventional contrastive learning is proposed to tailor these models.
The approach leverages an interventional dataset to focus the model's representations on task-relevant speech variability.

stories.title: ChLogic: Evaluating Robustness of Logical Reasoning in Chinese Expressions
stories.author: Xueyan Niu
bullet_points: Introduces ChLogic, a new benchmark designed to test the robustness of logical reasoning in Large Language Models (LLMs) across English and Chinese.
The benchmark specifically evaluates if LLMs maintain their logical reasoning performance when the same underlying logic is expressed in different Chinese surface realizations.
It is built from formal logical templates and includes a General aligned set, addressing the current gap in cross-lingual robustness testing for LLMs.

stories.title: Dynamic Rollout Editing for Reducing Overthinking in RL-Trained Reasoning Models
stories.author: Xueqi Cheng
bullet_points: Addresses the 'overthinking' problem in LLMs, where models continue unnecessary reasoning after reaching a correct answer.
Proposes a solution using Dynamic Rollout Editing framed within GRPO-style Reinforcement Learning (RL) post-training.
Treats the issue as a training-time credit-assignment problem rather than solely a decoding-time stopping mechanism.

stories.title: Environment-Grounded Automated Prompt Optimization for LLM Game Agents
stories.author: Matthias Feurer
bullet_points: Addresses the manual nature of prompt engineering for LLM agents in interactive environments.
Introduces an automated prompt optimization framework that uses an LLM-driven evolutionary loop.
The framework decomposes the observation-to-action pipeline into specialized descriptor and action selection agents.

stories.title: A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer
stories.author: roman.pt via hoistbypetard
bullet_points: The story concerns a security vulnerability or 'backdoor' found within a LinkedIn job offer.
This suggests a potential phishing or social engineering attack vector targeting job seekers.
The topic is relevant to cybersecurity and platform security, though the specific impact is not detailed in the snippet.

stories.title: Why the Fable 5 Crisis Proves Your AI Context Layer Can't Live Inside the Model
stories.author: jon_at_backboardio
bullet_points: The article discusses the 'Fable 5 Crisis' as a case study.
It argues against embedding AI context layers directly within the core model.
The central theme is advocating for separating intelligence (model) from memory (context layer).

stories.title: Your AI Provider Is a Single Point of Failure
stories.author: maishsk
bullet_points: The story addresses a critical infrastructure concern regarding AI providers.
The U.S. Commerce Department has taken regulatory action against Anthropic.
The issue highlights the risk of relying on a single AI vendor (Single Point of Failure).

stories.title: The $0 Bug That Cost Us $1,800 in API Calls
stories.author: arpitstack
bullet_points: A developer experienced a massive, unexplained spike in OpenAI API costs ($620 to $2,480 in 23 days).
The cost increase occurred without any corresponding new feature deployments, suggesting an underlying inefficiency or bug.
The story highlights the financial risks and operational challenges associated with relying heavily on external AI APIs.
